rationale: X's core finding — that both submit_initial and submit_update reject list prompts for diffusion, so unwrapping shouldn't be gated on already_submitted — almost exactly reproduces the ground-truth inline comment's reasoning, and its verdict (APPROVE, non-blocking suggestions) matches the actual two LGTM approvals. Y is verbose but self-contradicts (first says already_submitted 'remains used elsewhere — not dead', then recommends removing it as dead code), hedges with 'not performed due to budget'/'blocked by truncation', speculates about unverified fields (.finished/.request_id) and cites an unsubstantiated 'issue #4839' not grounded in the given PR context, all of which hurt precision and recall of the actual reviewer concern. X is more concise, better grounded, and more actionable relative to what a human reviewer actually cared about.
